description: You stand in a cracked asphalt lot outside a dusty Valero gas station. The neon sign flickers weakly in the relentless desert heat.
A long, sun-bleached road stretches in both directions through the flat desert. Heat shimmers rise from the asphalt in lazy waves.
A weathered stop sign marks this crossing of two crumbling roads. Tire tracks in the dust suggest it was busier once.
A rusted guardrail lines the road's edge here, bent and flaking. Beyond it, the desert drops away into a shallow arroyo.
An old parking lot, its surface cracked and heaved by decades of summer heat. Scraggly weeds push up through every fissure.
Dry, hard-packed earth stretches in all directions. A few brittle stalks of grass rattle in the hot wind.
A shallow creek bed winds through the desert here, bone dry and choked with pale gravel. It hasn't seen water in years.
A dense thicket of dark scrub brush crowds the path. Thorns catch at your clothes, and something unseen rustles in the shadows.
The skeletal remains of an old gas station lean in the heat. The pumps are long gone; only their concrete islands remain, stained and crumbling.

shortDescription: A Hill with a Desert Moose
description: A low rocky hill rises here, and at its crest stands a large moose, utterly unbothered by the desert surroundings, chewing something dry.
Gnarled mesquite trees cast thin, inadequate shade. Their roots snake across the surface of the ground, searching for any hidden moisture.
Large sandstone boulders jut from the desert floor here, warm to the touch and worn smooth by wind-driven sand over countless years.
An expanse of pale, cracked clay stretches to the south, its surface patterned like the scales of some enormous sleeping beast.
The air here shimmers with a persistent mirage — a glittering lake that retreats endlessly as you approach. The horizon seems to breathe.
A single enormous saguaro cactus stands here, its arms raised to the burning sky. Decades of growth captured in weathered green flesh.
From this slight rise, the jagged silhouette of distant mountains cuts the horizon. They look close but the desert between is merciless.
